_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I'm understanding the user's request now — they want me to act as a critic reviewing the PRD document against seven specific focus areas, and they're asking for findings with severity levels and recommendations rather than a rewrite. They've also tagged this with a session ID, so I should track this as a critique session in the context. me cross-check the brief requirements against what's in the PRD to see if anything's missing. All the core MUSTs look covered — affordability assessment, financial tracking, testing, edge cases, appropriate presentation, FCA compliance considerations, and the stretch items around currency, sharing, and PDF export. Now I need to verify that stretch items are properly scoped for testing as the brief specifies.

There's also a potential gap: the brief gives an example I&E shape but the PRD doesn't explicitly define what data structure customers use to input income and expenditure. The discovery notes assume the input mechanism already exists in the app, but R1 and R2 don't clarify where that data comes from or what format it takes. This might be intentionally deferred to the tech spec, but it's worth flagging as an ambiguity.

Looking at scope creep, R8 (the 7-persona fixture set) isn't mentioned in the brief at all — discovery references it as an internal implementation detail, yet it's coded as a Should requirement, which is odd for test fixtures. The category is also misaligned: it's labeled as Vulnerability but really belongs under Submission or Core. R7 (the signpost to human support) goes beyond what the brief explicitly asks for, though it's defensible as duty-of-care. R9 on explainability is properly covered by the brief.

On vulnerable-customer risks, R6 is vague about accessibility — discovery flagged screen-reader and motor accessibility as baseline expectations, but the PRD doesn't capture this at all. The bigger issue is how A1 handles zero income: it collapses it into the shortfall outcome, but a customer with zero income and non-zero spending is materially different from someone spending more than they earn. They need different messaging and support pathways. R7 mentions a signpost to human support but doesn't specify channels (phone, callback, etc.) — discovery says multiple channels and accessibility are baseline, but that's missing from the PRD. The joint-income household persona also raises FCA considerations around consent and financial abuse that aren't addressed.

On the self-declare mechanism: discovery mentions customers should be able to flag a need for extra support (health, bereavement, abuse, cognitive impairment), and R7 says never auto-label as vulnerable, but the PRD doesn't actually specify where or how a customer declares this need. It only says to signpost on shortfall/zero-income outcomes — the self-declare path is silent.

For FCA and data governance gaps: the PRD references Consumer Duty and UK GDPR in Section 3 but never specifies retention periods or what data minimisation means in scope. R10 says store only what the assessment needs, but doesn't address how long snapshots persist or whether that aligns with GDPR storage limitation. Customer rights to deletion and data portability aren't mentioned either. On explainability, R9 covers plain language but doesn't clarify how decisions are actually surfaced to the customer.

The brief warns against customers having "no sense of whether their repayment options are realistic," and the product surfaces a band and delta — R1 covers telling customers when a position is unaffordable, but the PRD doesn't capture whether this product actually delivers fair value beyond surfacing numbers. Consumer Duty framing is explicit in the brief and Section 3, but most requirements don't cite FCA reasoning except R9, R10, and partially R1; R6 and R7 cite brief lines but not the FCA context directly.

On R1 and R2: R1 defines a categorical band (surplus/breakeven/shortfall), but A1 says a "near-breakeven" band may be added by tech-spec if a small-positive DI feels misleadingly celebrated — so R1 contradicts itself on whether the band set is fixed or extensible. The PRD should commit to either three bands or three-plus-optional-fourth. R2 says each I&E submission creates a snapshot and the surface shows a delta versus the previous one, but "delta" is ambiguous — is it just disposable-income change, band change, or both?

R3 (view previously submitted snapshots) doesn't specify whether that's full history, last N, or customer-paginated. R4 borrows "tests protect the cases that actually matter" from the brief but never operationally defines what matters. R5 and R6 both aim for non-alarming, supportive tone without a tone guide or verification method. R7 says "clear, visible signpost to human support" but doesn't define what a signpost is — phone number, link, live chat, crisis line? Each has different operational implications. R8 (joint-income household) lacks a schema definition. R9 (plain language) doesn't specify a reading level standard. R10 (data minimisation) asks to store only what the assessment needs, but that's circular without defining what the assessment actually requires.

R11 (schema migrations) only applies if a database is chosen, which the PRD hasn't committed to. R12 (time-limited link) has no specified duration. R14 (fresh-clone reviewer can run without follow-up) is testable but doesn't name specific environments.

The traceability table maps discovery back to the PRD, which is fine — forward mapping to the tech-spec is the next phase's job. Internal cross-references check out: Q1/A1's relationship to the default band is clear, and the notes references to vulnerable customers and auto-classification exist. But the regulatory constraints (Consumer Duty, FG21/1, CONC, UK GDPR) mentioned in section 3 aren't tagged in any requirement's "Why" column, so they're floating as context rather than tied to specific requirements.

R8 does have discovery backing for the joint-income aspect. However, the brief's line about stretch items needing testing isn't captured in any requirement — R4 doesn't extend to stretches, and R11-R13 don't mandate tests. Also, the immutable snapshot model means customers can't edit past submissions, only create new ones, but the PRD doesn't address how corrections flow through the system. R5's validation for invalid input (negatives, non-numeric) implies a validation layer, but the PRD doesn't specify whether that's client-side or server-side. The joint-income household requirement in R8 signals a more complex I&E schema than single-customer, which the tech-spec will need to design but the PRD doesn't flag as a schema implication.

R12's security model for time-limited share links is completely undefined — whether they're anonymous or customer-bound is left to implementation. R10's prohibition on PII in logs is hard to enforce without explicit instrumentation and a log scrubber. R6's "no dark patterns" is vague at the implementation level and easy to violate accidentally. R4's test coverage doesn't explicitly bind to R8's fixture set, so the tech-spec will need to fill that gap. The PRD assumes English as the default language but never states it. Migration safety in R11 assumes a database schema exists; if storage changes later, R11 loses meaning. And there's a real risk that if a stretch item is attempted, the tech-spec might not realize R4 doesn't cover it, leading to untested code shipping while still technically complying with the requirements.

The PRD also labels stakeholders as "Primary" and "Tertiary" but skips "Secondary" — the table in Section 2 only shows those two, which mirrors the notes but creates odd labelling.

The self-declare path for vulnerability segments is mentioned in Section 2 but never actually required in Section 6 — only R7 signposts it. And R8's personas include "new customer (no I&E yet)" but R8 is a Should, not a Must, so if it's dropped, R5's no-data state becomes untestable. The dependency chain between R4, R5, and R8 isn't solid.

I have enough findings now. Let me organize them by the seven categories the user asked for — finding, severity, recommendation — then wrap up with the verdict and top three fixes. I'll follow the critic spec: no file updates, no invented regulatory citations, only what's in the discovery and PRD.

I'm staying read-only for this turn and will deliver the review first, then offer session housekeeping if needed.</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

I'll review `docs/PRD.md` as the critic — read-only, no rewrites. Upstream inputs (`docs/Ophelos Engineering Take-Home Task.pdf` and `docs/discovery/NOTES.md`) are present and load-bearing, so the review proceeds.

---

# Critic review — `docs/PRD.md`

**Verdict:** **Minor fixes.** Brief coverage is essentially complete and the phase-gate hygiene (append-only IDs, labelled paraphrases, paired Q/A) is solid. The PRD has a small number of real gaps — most importantly accessibility, joint-income economic-abuse risk, and a category-mismatch on R8 — that are append-only fixes, not a rewrite.

---

## 1. Missing requirements from the brief

### F1.1 — Brief line 75 ("Stretch items must be well tested if attempted") is not bound to any requirement
- **Finding:** R4 ("tests protect the cases that actually matter") is scoped via R5/R8 (edge cases + personas). R11/R12/R13 (the stretches) carry no `tests-when-attempted` obligation. A reviewer could read this as "stretches ship without tests and still pass R4". The brief explicitly forbids this (line 75).
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** Either extend R4's scope to "including any Stretch requirement that is actually delivered", or add a separate `R*` row to bind R11–R13 to test coverage if attempted. Append-only — no renumbering.

### F1.2 — The act of I&E input is implicit, not required
- **Finding:** R2 says each "I&E submission creates an immutable snapshot" and R1 names disposable income / band, but **nothing in §6 commits to the customer being able to *enter* I&E at all**. Discovery §1 ("customers can already log their income and outgoings") treats it as pre-existing; the brief example data (lines 47–52) is "shape, not schema" (line 53). The implicit assumption makes R5 case (d) ("invalid input") unanchored — invalid input to *what*?
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** Add a Must (`R*`) for "the customer can submit / refresh their I&E" — or explicitly state in a non-goal / assumption that I&E input is fixture-driven for the take-home and R5(d) refers to fixture-validation rather than a user-facing form.

### F1.3 — No requirement captures the self-declare "I need extra support" path
- **Finding:** PRD §2 lists "Customers who self-declare a need for extra support (health, bereavement, abuse, cognitive impairment indicators)" as a vulnerability segment. Discovery §2 names self-declaration as the safer alternative to inference. **No `R*` requirement actually provides a self-declare affordance.** R7 provides only a one-way signpost *out* to human support. R7 ≠ self-declare.
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** Either add an explicit `R*` for a self-declare affordance, or move "self-declare segment" out of §2 with a note that it is honoured purely via R7's signpost (and accept the conduct risk that the system never lets the user say "I need help" *before* hitting an outcome screen).

---

## 2. Scope creep

### F2.1 — R8 (7-persona fixture set) is mis-categorised as `Vulnerability`
- **Finding:** R8 is fixture infrastructure — load-bearing for R4 (per Notes column on R8). It is not a vulnerability commitment to the customer. Putting it under `Vulnerability` inflates the vulnerability category and obscures that R8 is a *test enabler*, not a user-facing duty-of-care commitment.
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** Recategorise R8 as `Core` (it backstops R4) or introduce a `Submission`/`Test-infrastructure` category. Or, more defensibly, demote it: PRD-level should describe persona *coverage* in prose under R4's Why, and let tech-spec / test-plan own the fixture itself. The template's anti-patterns warn against test-infrastructure leaking into PRD requirements.

### F2.2 — R7 ("clear, visible signpost to human support on every outcome screen") is brief-adjacent, not brief-mandated
- **Finding:** The brief asks for "fair treatment of vulnerable customers" and "appropriate" presentation (lines 67–68). Discovery §2 introduces the signpost as an FG21/1-spirit paraphrase. R7 is defensible as a duty-of-care derivative, but it is the PRD's *interpretation*, not a brief MUST. The current `Why` column cites brief lines 17–19, 67–70 — those lines do not literally require a signpost.
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** Keep R7 but reframe its `Why` so the reviewer sees it is a discovery-derived duty-of-care commitment, not a brief literal. The current citation reads tighter than it actually is.

---

## 3. Vulnerable-customer risks

### F3.1 — Accessibility (screen-reader / motor / cognitive) is completely missing from §6
- **Finding:** Discovery §2 says "Multiple channels (phone, callback) and **screen-reader / motor accessibility are baseline expectations, not stretch**." The PRD does not mention accessibility in any requirement, in §3, or as a non-goal. This is the most material vulnerability blind spot in the document.
- **Severity:** **High.**
- **Recommendation:** Either add a Should-class `R*` ("the product meets a named accessibility baseline — e.g. WCAG 2.2 AA at PRD level, target details deferred to tech-spec"), or explicitly mark accessibility as `Won't` for the take-home and justify it. Silence is not safe given the brief's FCA framing.

### F3.2 — A1 collapses "income = 0" into the shortfall band, conflating two materially different vulnerability profiles
- **Finding:** R5 lists "income = 0" as a *distinct* first-class case. A1 then says "Income = 0 (R5 case a) routes to the **shortfall outcome** unless expenditure is also 0." A zero-income customer is not the same as an over-spending customer — they may be between jobs, in a benefits delay, recently bereaved, or fleeing economic abuse. Bundling them into shortfall messaging risks both wrong tone and missed signposting.
- **Severity:** **High.**
- **Recommendation:** Either treat zero-income as its own outcome (4 outcomes, not 3 bands) or have A1 explicitly say "shortfall outcome with a zero-income variant message and a stronger support signpost". Don't quietly merge them.

### F3.3 — "Joint-income household" persona (R8 / §6) introduces economic-abuse risk that the PRD does not acknowledge
- **Finding:** Joint-income households are a known FCA vulnerability surface (one party may be the source of financial abuse; consent to data entry by one party doesn't imply consent by the other). PRD adopts the persona from discovery §6 OQ-6 without acknowledging this risk — and §6 of the PRD has no requirement for handling joint-account vulnerability indicators.
- **Severity:** Medium (would be High in a production PRD; medium here because the take-home is fixture-only).
- **Recommendation:** Add a one-line constraint to §3 or a note under R8 acknowledging that joint-income is a flagged vulnerability surface and that the take-home does not attempt to address economic-abuse signals. Be explicit about the limitation rather than silent about it.

### F3.4 — R6 ("supportive, non-judgemental tone…") and R7 ("clear, visible signpost…") have no testable definition
- **Finding:** Both are subjective. R6 names four negatives ("no collections-aggressive language; no nudging into unaffordable positions; no dark patterns") without a positive specification. R7 says "signpost" without saying *what* (phone, link, live chat, Samaritans, internal COps line). Different signposts have different operational and conduct implications.
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** Either (a) bind R6 to a named tone guide / reading level at PRD level, or (b) explicitly defer tone-guide selection to tech-spec / implementation and accept the test-plan will assert only weak properties (e.g. absence of named anti-patterns). For R7, name the signpost class (e.g. "phone number + one named external support route") at PRD level rather than leaving it for tech-spec to guess.

---

## 4. FCA-context gaps

### F4.1 — UK GDPR retention is named in §3 but absent from R10
- **Finding:** §3 cites "UK GDPR data minimisation — paraphrased" and labels it. R10 implements only the *minimisation* half ("store only what the assessment needs; no PII or I&E line items in logs"). UK GDPR also imposes **storage limitation** (retention). R2/R3 commit to keeping snapshots indefinitely (no retention named). The PRD has no requirement, non-goal, or assumption that touches snapshot retention.
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** Either add an assumption (A4) that "snapshots are retained for the lifetime of the customer record in this take-home; production retention is out of scope" or add a Should-class `R*` capping retention. Silence reads as oversight given §3 specifically named UK GDPR.

### F4.2 — Consumer Duty, FG21/1, CONC are named in §3 but tagged on zero requirements' `Why` columns
- **Finding:** §3 lists four paraphrased FCA / regulatory bullets. None of the four are cited in the `Why` column of any `R*`. R9 cites brief lines 19, 69–70 (explainability) and R10 cites lines 18, 69–70 (data minimisation), but not the §3 paraphrases by name. The §3 stance therefore floats — a reviewer cannot trace which `R*` satisfies "Consumer Duty good outcomes" or "FG21/1 spirit".
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** Tag each §3 paraphrase with the `R*` IDs that operationalise it (e.g. Consumer Duty → R1, R6, R7, R9; FG21/1 → R5, R7; CONC posture → R6; GDPR → R10). The mapping can sit in §9 traceability or as a sub-table inside §3.

### F4.3 — "How decisions are surfaced" (brief line 70) is honoured by R9 (explainability) but the *uncertainty* of the assessment is not addressed
- **Finding:** Brief line 32 says "no sense of whether their repayment options are realistic" — discovery §1 calls for honesty when a position is unaffordable. The PRD covers the *band* and the *delta* but never commits to telling the customer "this is an indicative read, not a promise / financial advice". For an FCA-authorised firm surfacing an affordability verdict, the absence of an "indicative / not financial advice" framing is a conduct risk.
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** Add a Should-class `R*` ("the affordability output is framed as indicative / not financial advice") or fold the principle into R9. Suspect-only — this is not a cited FCA clause; the critic is naming it as a suspicion per the critic's anti-fabrication rule.

---

## 5. Requirement ambiguity

### F5.1 — R1 bands vs A1 "near-breakeven" optional 4th band
- **Finding:** R1 declares "a categorical band (surplus / breakeven / shortfall)". A1 says tech-spec / implementation *may* add a "near-breakeven" band. The requirement and the working assumption disagree on the band set.
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** Either pin the band set at PRD level (3 bands, optional 4th is out-of-scope) or restate R1 as "at least three bands; tech-spec may add a near-breakeven band." Append-only.

### F5.2 — R2 "delta vs the previous snapshot" — delta of *what*?
- **Finding:** R2 names a delta but does not say whether the delta is disposable-income £, band-change, both, or something else. Tech-spec cannot pick without re-litigating PRD scope.
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** Specify in R2's statement (e.g. "…delta in disposable income and band classification vs the previous snapshot…") or pin it in A2/Q2.

### F5.3 — R3 "previously submitted snapshots" — how many, ordered how, paginated how?
- **Finding:** R3 commits to retrieval but not to depth (all? last N?) or order.
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** A one-line clarification: "all snapshots for the customer, most-recent first" or similar. Append-only.

### F5.4 — R8 "joint-income household" schema is undefined
- **Finding:** R8 names seven personas. "Joint-income household" can mean two earners on one I&E, two named I&E inputs combined, or shared expenses with a single earner. Tech-spec / fixtures cannot pick without invention.
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** Either pin the joint-income schema at PRD level (one or two I&E line-shapes) or move the persona narrative to a footnote and demote R8 to "the fixture set covers these classes" rather than naming personas.

### F5.5 — R6, R9, R10 lean on subjective adjectives without testable anchors
- **Finding:** "Supportive", "plain language", "data minimisation" are all unfalsifiable in their current form. The test plan will be forced either to invent acceptance criteria or to assert only weak properties.
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** Note in each requirement's `Why` (or as a footnote) that the operational acceptance criteria are deferred to test-plan, and that the PRD-level commitment is the *direction*, not the threshold. This is honest about the ambiguity rather than pretending otherwise.

---

## 6. Traceability gaps

### F6.1 — `Secondary` user is missing from §2 table
- **Finding:** §2 table jumps from "Primary" to "Tertiary". Discovery §2 has a "Secondary" tier (the reviewer), which the PRD's §2 table re-labels as "Tertiary". Either §2 is missing the Secondary row, or the labelling is off-by-one against discovery.
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** Either renumber the reviewer to "Secondary" (matching discovery) or add a row noting that there is no Secondary in this product and the reviewer is the Tertiary user by design.

### F6.2 — §3 paraphrases are not back-linked from any `R*` (see F4.2)
- **Finding:** Same finding from a traceability angle: §3 bullets terminate; nothing in §6 cites them.
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** As F4.2.

### F6.3 — No forward-link slot for tech-spec `S*` IDs
- **Finding:** The §9 traceability table maps discovery → PRD. The PRD has no placeholder for tech-spec to fill in `S*` against each `R*`. Per `.cursor/rules/10-evidence.mdc` "stable identifiers", tech-spec carries `Requirements: R…` per-section, so the reverse mapping is feasible — but the PRD doesn't reserve a column or follow-on table for it.
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** Optional — a `Tech-spec coverage` column to be filled by `/tech-spec`. The phase-gate skill may already cover this; if so, ignore.

### F6.4 — `§2` vulnerability segment "self-declare extra support" has no `R*` in §6 (see F1.3)
- **Finding:** Same finding from a traceability angle.
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** As F1.3.

---

## 7. Future implementation risks

### F7.1 — R5 case (d) "invalid input" assumes a user-facing input surface that no `R*` commits to (see F1.2)
- **Finding:** Implementation could legitimately ship with fixture-only I&E (no input form), at which point R5(d) is moot. Tech-spec will have to guess whether to build input.
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** As F1.2 — either commit to a customer-facing input surface or scope R5(d) to fixture-validation.

### F7.2 — "Immutable snapshot" model (R2) has no correction story
- **Finding:** If a customer submits a typo (e.g. £28,000 salary instead of £2,800), the immutable model means the bad snapshot stays forever and the next snapshot will read as a massive shortfall delta. The PRD doesn't say whether corrections are a new snapshot ("amend by re-submitting") or whether a snapshot can be marked invalid.
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** A one-line note in R2 or A2 — e.g. "corrections are made by submitting a new snapshot; previous snapshots remain visible". Or accept the implementation invents this behaviour.

### F7.3 — R10 "no PII or I&E line items in logs" is hard to enforce without an explicit logging discipline
- **Finding:** The PRD states the obligation but offers no implementation hook (e.g. a named log scrubber, a structured-logging schema, a denylist). Implementation may legitimately interpret this as "be careful" and ship a system that violates R10 in error paths.
- **Severity:** Medium.
- **Recommendation:** This is a tech-spec / implementation concern; PRD should not pre-specify the mechanism. But the test plan will need an `R10`-coverage test (e.g. "no I&E lines in test logs"), and R4 should be the natural home. Worth flagging to the test-plan author rather than amending the PRD.

### F7.4 — R12 (time-limited share link) has no security model at PRD level
- **Finding:** "Time-limited" is unbounded (24h? 7d?), and there is no commitment to whether the link is customer-bound, anonymous, single-use, etc. Tech-spec would have to invent the threat model.
- **Severity:** Low (Could-class, MVP-deferred).
- **Recommendation:** Even if R12 stays Could, add a one-line note: "single-use link with a default expiry to be set by tech-spec" — gives tech-spec the smallest reasonable hook.

### F7.5 — N7 (no multi-language UI) leaves default language unspecified
- **Finding:** N7 defers Welsh. The PRD never says the default is English. Take-home-trivial, but it's an implementation-risk because nothing in the PRD lets the reviewer challenge "why is this English-only?".
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** Add to N7 or as A4: "default UI language is English-UK for this take-home." One line.

### F7.6 — R8's dependency chain (R4 ← R8 ← R5) is informal
- **Finding:** R4's `Why` mentions R5 but not R8. R8's Notes mention R4 but R4's row doesn't cite R8. If R8 is dropped (it's Should), R5 loses its test fixtures and R4's coverage scope quietly shrinks. The dependency is fragile.
- **Severity:** Low.
- **Recommendation:** A single sentence in R4 or a Notes row binding R4 to R5 + R8 jointly: "R4 covers, at minimum, every R5 case and every R8 persona."

---

## Top three things to fix first

1. **F3.1 (accessibility gap)** — add a Should-class `R*` for an accessibility baseline (WCAG 2.2 AA at PRD level, target details deferred), or explicitly `Won't` accessibility with a justification. **Highest severity, structural absence.**
2. **F3.2 (zero-income collapsed into shortfall in A1)** — split zero-income from shortfall, either as its own band/outcome or as a zero-income variant of shortfall with distinct messaging and stronger signposting. **Conduct risk.**
3. **F1.3 + F6.4 (self-declare path missing)** — either add an `R*` for a self-declare "I need extra support" affordance, or remove the "self-declare" segment from §2 and acknowledge in §3 that the take-home honours self-declaration purely via R7's signpost. **Traceability gap with conduct flavour.**

Honourable mentions worth doing in the same revision pass, all append-only: F1.1 (bind stretches to R4), F2.1 (recategorise R8 out of Vulnerability), F4.1 (retention assumption), F5.1 (band-set ambiguity), F7.2 (immutable-snapshot correction story).

---

Note on housekeeping: per the `ai-history` user rule, an S005 session snapshot under `docs/ai/sessions/` and a row in `docs/PROMPT_HISTORY.md` would record this critique pass. The critic role is read-only, so I have not created them — say the word if you want me to switch out of critic stance and lay them down.

7. Do not modify discovery artefacts.

- explain why the change resolves it
- show the exact append-only modification

 I need to address four specific findings with minimal changes. For the accessibility gap, I should add a baseline requirement class. The stretch items need testing validation if they're implemented. For F3.1, discovery explicitly names screen-reader and motor accessibility as baseline expectations, plus multiple channels. I'll add a new requirement binding to those discovery commitments without inventing a WCAG version that isn't cited. For F1.1, I'll create a separate requirement linking the stretch items to test coverage requirements rather than modifying an existing one. For F4.1, I need to surface the retention assumption that's implicit in the discovery. For F7.2, I'll correct the immutable snapshot story to align with what discovery actually says. Now I'm working through the traceability table updates in section 9. R18 (accessibility) maps back to the discovery note about screen-reader and motor accessibility being baseline expectations, while R19 (stretch tests) derives from brief line 75 about testing stretch items if attempted. I need to figure out whether R19 belongs in the Core or Submission category — it's a constraint on the test suite, so Core makes sense, though Submission could work too depending on how we frame it.

For the open questions section, I'm adding Q4/A4 about retention and Q5/A5 about snapshot corrections. The success metrics in section 7 don't need updates since R18 and R19 don't map cleanly to the existing goals G1-G4, and keeping changes minimal means leaving those alone.

Now I'm drafting the actual requirement rows. R18 covers accessibility as a Should-priority Vulnerability category, grounded in the FCA fair treatment language and the brief's note that screen-reader and motor accessibility are baseline expectations. For R19, I'm wrestling with whether it should be Must or Should — the brief says "must be well tested" which sounds imperative, but it's conditional on stretches being delivered, so Should feels more accurate for the priority level. The category is Core since it's the testing discipline that mirrors R4.

For the retention question, I'm adding Q4 to ask what snapshot retention period applies, since the brief mentions UK GDPR storage limitation but doesn't specify a value. The corresponding assumption A4 clarifies that snapshots stay for the customer record's lifetime in this take-home, with production retention rules out of scope.

Now for Q5 and A5 on corrections — since snapshots are immutable, the approach is to submit a new snapshot rather than edit the old one; prior snapshots remain visible, and the implementation can optionally flag when a delta looks like a correction.

I'm also updating the traceability table to add rows for the accessibility baseline and the stretch testing requirement, then double-checking that brief-sourced items like R19 fit the same pattern as existing rows even when not anchored in NOTES.md.

The gate criteria about pairing open questions with working assumptions is satisfied by adding Q4/A4 and Q5/A5 as matched sets. The trailing note about append-only IDs and no drops remains accurate since R18 and R19 are first-issue additions, not removals.

The summary note at line 95 about first-issue IDs still holds — S004 issued R1-R17, S005 will issue R18-R19. Now I'm preparing the exact markdown table rows for R18 and R19, along with the corresponding Q/A pairs for snapshot retention and correction workflows. _**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

I'll switch out of critic stance to make the four append-only PRD edits. Before doing so, here is the change set with traceback to the findings, so you can sanity-check each one before it lands.

## Change set (4 edits, all append-only)

### Edit 1 — Add R18 (accessibility) and R19 (stretch-test discipline) to §6 Requirements

**Resolves:** F3.1 (R18), F1.1 (R19).

**Why it resolves them:**
- **R18** binds the PRD to discovery §2's "screen-reader / motor accessibility are baseline expectations, not stretch" without pre-committing to a WCAG level (which neither the brief nor discovery cite — that would be fabrication). Tech-spec picks the conformance target.
- **R19** binds any delivered Stretch (R11/R12/R13) to R4's test discipline, mirroring brief line 75 ("Stretch items must be well tested if attempted") without editing R4.

**Exact addition** (inserted immediately after R17, before the drop-policy note):

```text
| R18 | The customer can use the product with a screen reader and without precise motor input; the specific accessibility standard and conformance level are chosen by tech-spec. | Should | Vulnerability | brief lines 17–19 (FCA fair treatment), 67–68 (appropriate presentation); `NOTES.md` §2 Vulnerable customers ("screen-reader / motor accessibility are baseline expectations, not stretch") |
| R19 | If any Stretch requirement (R11, R12, R13) is delivered, it is covered by automated tests of the same standard as R4 — protecting real cases, not happy-path-only. | Should | Core | brief line 75 ("Stretch items must be well tested if attempted") |
```

### Edit 2 — Add Q4 (retention) and Q5 (snapshot correction) to §8 Open questions

**Resolves:** F4.1 (Q4), F7.2 (Q5).

**Why it resolves them:** the PRD-template's gate criterion "every open question has a paired working assumption that can be adopted if it stays unresolved" is the cleanest append-only home for both items. They are not new product scope — they are clarifications of behaviour already implicit in R2/R3.

**Exact addition** (after Q3 in the Open questions list):

```text
- **Q4** — What snapshot retention period applies? (`NOTES.md` §5 names UK GDPR storage limitation but does not pin a value; §3 paraphrases it but §6 does not address retention.)
- **Q5** — How does the customer correct a snapshot containing an input mistake, given snapshots are immutable (R2)?
```

### Edit 3 — Add A4 (retention default) and A5 (correction story) to §8 Working assumptions

**Resolves:** F4.1 (A4), F7.2 (A5).

**Why it resolves them:** these are the adopted-if-unresolved defaults that close out Q4 and Q5 without expanding scope or editing existing requirements.

**Exact addition** (after A3 in the Working assumptions list):

```text
- **A4** — Snapshots are retained for the lifetime of the customer record in this take-home; production retention rules are out of scope and consistent with N8 (independent verification of FCA / GDPR citations out of scope). Tech-spec / implementation revisit only if a Stretch (R11–R13) later forces the question.
- **A5** — Corrections are made by submitting a new snapshot; previous snapshots remain visible and are not edited, hidden, or deleted. The next snapshot's delta (R2) vs an incorrect prior snapshot may read unusually large; tech-spec / implementation choose whether to surface a "this looks like a correction" affordance.
```

### Edit 4 — Add traceability rows for R18 and R19 to §9

**Resolves:** keeps Phase 2 → Phase 3 traceability intact for the two new requirements.

**Why it resolves it:** the phase-gate convention requires every `R*` to appear in §9. Q4/A4 and Q5/A5 do not need traceability rows (they are PRD-internal scaffolding, like the existing Q1/A1 etc.).

**Exact addition** (after the existing "Submission artefacts" row, before the OQ-1 housekeeping row):

```text
| Accessibility baseline — screen-reader and motor accessibility (`NOTES.md` §2 Vulnerable customers) | R18 | Should; the specific accessibility standard and conformance level are chosen by tech-spec. |
| Brief line 75 — "Stretch items must be well tested if attempted" | R19 | Should; conditional on any of R11, R12, R13 being delivered. Extends R4's discipline to delivered stretches without editing R4. |
```

Applying the edits now.

## Summary of what was added

| Critic finding | PRD addition | Section |
|---|---|---|
| F3.1 — Accessibility gap | **R18** (Should / Vulnerability) | §6 Requirements |
| F1.1 — Stretch tests if implemented | **R19** (Should / Core) | §6 Requirements |
| F4.1 — Retention assumption | **Q4 / A4** pair | §8 Open questions + Working assumptions |
| F7.2 — Snapshot correction story | **Q5 / A5** pair | §8 Open questions + Working assumptions |
| Traceability for R18 + R19 | 2 new rows | §9 Traceability table |

**Constraints honoured:**

- All 17 pre-existing requirement IDs (R1–R17) are byte-for-byte unchanged.
- R18 and R19 are first-issue and append-only; no IDs reused or renumbered.
- Q1–Q3 / A1–A3 are unchanged; Q4–Q5 / A4–A5 are appended.
- No new product scope: R18 reflects discovery §2 verbatim phrasing; R19 reflects brief line 75 verbatim phrasing; A4/A5 are PRD-internal defaults, not new features.
- PRD remains implementation-agnostic: R18 defers conformance level to tech-spec; A4 defers retention specifics to tech-spec; A5 defers "correction hint" UI to tech-spec.
- Discovery artefacts (`docs/discovery/NOTES.md`) were not modified.
- §3, §4, §5, §7 are unchanged.

---

## Critic findings intentionally deferred to the tech-spec

These are real findings, but they belong downstream — either because they are operational thresholds the PRD cannot pin without inventing, or because they are tech-spec/test-plan responsibilities. Tech-spec should pick each one up explicitly:

| Finding | Why deferred | Tech-spec hook |
|---|---|---|
| F2.1 — R8 mis-categorised as Vulnerability | Recategorisation rewrites an existing row (violates user-rule #1). Functionally, tech-spec will own fixture infrastructure under R4 + R8 jointly. | §S* covering R4/R8 makes the dependency explicit. |
| F2.2 — R7 brief-adjacency | Rewriting `Why` columns is also an existing-row edit. Section 3 already labels duty-of-care content as paraphrased. | No tech-spec action required; flag only. |
| F3.2 — A1 collapses zero-income into shortfall | Pinning a distinct zero-income outcome edits A1 (an existing row). | Tech-spec can choose to add a zero-income variant message under R5(a) / R7 without re-opening A1. |
| F3.3 — Joint-income economic-abuse risk | Adding this would expand product scope (a new vulnerability surface). User explicitly excluded new scope. | Tech-spec records as an out-of-scope limitation under §S* for R8. |
| F3.4 — R6 / R7 testable definitions | These tighten R4 / R5 / R6 / R7 rather than add scope. | Test-plan responsibility — R6 / R7 acceptance criteria. |
| F4.2 — §3 paraphrases not tagged on R-rows | Tagging would edit `Why` columns of multiple existing requirements. | Tech-spec / test-plan can build a §3 → R* matrix without touching the PRD. |
| F4.3 — "indicative / not financial advice" framing | Adding this would create a new product behaviour (a disclaimer surface). New scope. | Tech-spec can propose this under R1 / R9 design and route back through the PRD if accepted. |
| F5.1 — R1 / A1 band-set ambiguity | A1 already authorises the 4th band; pinning either way edits R1 or A1. | Tech-spec Q1 resolution. |
| F5.2 — R2 "delta of what" | Edits R2. | Tech-spec defines snapshot diff shape under R2. |
| F5.3 — R3 retrieval depth/order | Edits R3. | Tech-spec defines history-view contract under R3. |
| F5.4 — R8 joint-income schema | Edits R8. | Tech-spec / fixture work under R8. |
| F5.5 — Subjective adjectives in R6 / R9 / R10 | Tightening edits existing rows. | Test-plan responsibility. |
| F6.1 — "Secondary" user row missing from §2 table | Edits an existing section. Cosmetic. | Optional fix in a separate PRD touch-up session. |
| F6.3 — Forward-link slot for `S*` IDs | The phase-gate skill already requires tech-spec sections to cite `R*`; no PRD-side change needed. | Tech-spec §7 traceability table fills the reverse mapping. |
| F1.3 / F6.4 — Self-declare "I need extra support" path | Adding a self-declare affordance is **new product scope**, which the user excluded. The current PRD wording in §2 ("Customers who self-declare a need for extra support") is now best read as a *segment description*, not a feature commitment; R7 honours the segment via signposting. | Tech-spec can flag this as an out-of-scope limitation under §S* covering R7. |
| F7.1 — Implicit I&E input surface | F1.2 from the original review. Resolving it requires either a new `R*` (new scope) or an edit to R5 (existing row). | Tech-spec must decide whether I&E is customer-entered or fixture-loaded under R1 / R2; record under §S* for R2. |
| F7.3 — R10 logging discipline enforcement | Tech-spec / test-plan concern. | Tech-spec §cross-cutting concerns (data hygiene); test-plan R10 coverage. |
| F7.4 — R12 share-link security model | Could-class; tech-spec only addresses if R12 is attempted. | Tech-spec §S* for R12 if delivered. |
| F7.5 — N7 default language unstated | Trivially solvable in tech-spec without PRD edit. | Tech-spec assumes English-UK under §cross-cutting concerns. |
| F7.6 — R4 ↔ R5 ↔ R8 dependency informal | Cosmetic; test-plan will encode the dependency. | Test-plan explicitly binds R4 coverage to R5 cases + R8 personas. |

| Gate criterion | Status |
|---|---|
| All Must requirements have stable IDs and a `Why` linking to brief or discovery | R1–R4, R14–R17 unchanged and compliant; R18 / R19 are Should not Must (gate unaffected). |
| Non-goals list is non-empty and explicit | Unchanged: N1–N8 present. |
| FCA / vulnerability stance captured as constraints (§3) and as Should-class requirements (§6) | §3 unchanged; R5–R10 + new R18 reinforce the Should-class vulnerability surface. |
| Every open question has a paired working assumption that can be adopted if unresolved | Q1↔A1, Q2↔A2, Q3↔A3, **Q4↔A4 (new), Q5↔A5 (new)** — all paired. |
| Every requirement appears in §9 traceability | R1–R17 unchanged; **R18, R19 added** to §9. |

The `/tech-spec` pre-checks (`.rulesync/commands/tech-spec.md`) also pass: `docs/PRD.md` exists, has at least one Must requirement, and all open questions affecting architecture / data / external integrations are either resolved or paired with an adopt-as-default assumption (Q4/A4 explicitly defers retention-policy specifics to tech-spec / implementation; Q5/A5 pins the correction model at PRD level).
